coredata.dc:title: Surface plasmon resonance optical fiber sensor for relative humidity detection without temperature crosstalk
coredata.dc:creator: Tong, Ruijie
Wang, Yu
Zhao, Kai-jun
Li, Xiang
Zhao, Yong
coredata.dc:description: A new method to overcome temperature crosstalk in humidity detection is proposed in this manuscript, which is realized by integrated surface plasmon resonance (SPR) and Mach-Zehnder (MZ) interference in the same optical fiber detection structure. The proposed sensor is made by photonic crystal fiber and multimode fiber, SPR excited by half coated gold film on one side of photonic crystal fiber, MZ interference is made by photonic crystal fiber without gold film coating and multimode fiber. Graphene quantum dots and polyvinyl alcohol (GQDs-PVA) are adopted as relative humidity sensitive materials. By data demodulation, the sensitivity of proposed sensor to relative humidity is 23 pm/%RH. Compared with other RH sensors, the proposed sensor in this manuscript is high integrated, and overcomes the problems of complex data demodulation, which provides a novel idea for the sensor to overcome crosstalk of other factors.
coredata.dcterms:subject: SPR
MZ
GQDs-PVA
Relative humidity
Temperature

comparative with the proposed sensor with recently reported schemes.

head.ce:title: Surface plasmon resonance optical fiber sensor for relative humidity detection without temperature crosstalk

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Information Science and Engineering, Northeastern University, Shenyang 110004, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Information Science and Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Information Science and Engineering, Northeastern University, Shenyang 110004, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Hebei Key Laboratory of Micro-Nano Precision Optical Sensing and Measurement Technology, Qinhuangdao 066004, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Hebei Key Laboratory of Micro-Nano Precision Optical Sensing and Measurement Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Hebei Key Laboratory of Micro-Nano Precision Optical Sensing and Measurement Technology, Qinhuangdao 066004, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A new method to overcome temperature crosstalk in humidity detection is proposed in this manuscript, which is realized by integrated surface plasmon resonance (SPR) and Mach-Zehnder (MZ) interference in the same optical fiber detection structure. The proposed sensor is made by photonic crystal fiber and multimode fiber, SPR excited by half coated gold film on one side of photonic crystal fiber, MZ interference is made by photonic crystal fiber without gold film coating and multimode fiber. Graphene quantum dots and polyvinyl alcohol (GQDs-PVA) are adopted as relative humidity sensitive materials. By data demodulation, the sensitivity of proposed sensor to relative humidity is 23 pm/%RH. Compared with other RH sensors, the proposed sensor in this manuscript is high integrated, and overcomes the problems of complex data demodulation, which provides a novel idea for the sensor to overcome crosstalk of other factors.

ce:section.ce:para: Relative humidity (RH), as a physical quantity of atmospheric dryness, has important influences on many areas, such as atmospheric relative humidity environment [1], medicine production and preservation environment [2], the preservation of precision instruments [3–5] and so on [6]. Over the past years, relative humidity (RH) sensors based on optical fiber have been proposed due to the significant advantages such as highly chemically resistant [7], anti-electromagnetic interference [8], remote monitoring [9] and so on [10]. RH sensors realized by fiber Bragg grating [11], long period fiber grating [12], photonic crystal fiber [13] and interferometers [14] have been enough researched. While, temperature and RH are closely related and influence each other. In order to overcome the problem of temperature crosstalk in relative humidity, different methods have been proposed that can be divided into three categories. The first method, as the easiest way, is to cascade RH sensing element with fiber Bragg grating (FBG) to eliminate temperature influence [15], while FBG sensing element isn’t very sensitive to temperature. The second method generally adopts one optical interference [16], where the intensity and phase of the interference spectrum change with the refractive index changing, and the measured spectrum is characterized by intensity and phase of interference spectrum respectively, while spectrum intensity is influenced by stability of light source which introduce an irremediable experimental error. The third method is to connect two sensing structure in series, which will increase the transmission loss and may lead to spectral distortion. What’s more, the superposition of signals will increase the difficulty of later data demodulation. In addition, the two fiber sensing structures in cascade need to be tapered or corroded to reduce the mechanical properties of the sensing structure. In the past decades, SPR and Mach-Zehnder (MZ) interference detecting methods based on optical fiber have drawn researchers’ attention due to its high sensitivity to the change of refractive index [17,18], remote measurement [19,20], real time monitoring and so on [21,22], and plenty of sensors based on SPR or MZ interference have been presented.

In this manuscript, a sensing structure integrates SPR and MZ interference in one sensing element is proposed and adopted for RH detection without temperature crosstalk. The sensing structure composes of a segment of photonic crystal fiber (PCF) fused with multimode fiber (MMF) at both ends. What’s more, the one side of PCF is gold coated to generate SPR effect, and the other side without gold film can form MZ interference, therefore, SPR and MZ interference can be integrated in a sensing structure. Moreover, graphene quantum dots and polyvinyl alcohol (GQDs-PVA) is deposited on the surface of PCF. The refractive index and volume of GQDs-PVA is tunable by RH, which result in the output spectrum of proposed sensor change with the variation of RH and temperature. The sensitivity of proposed sensor to temperature and relative humidity is 102 pm/℃ and 23 pm/%RH respectively.

SPR technology and MZ interference are based on different sensing principle. How to integrate the two sensing methods into one optical fiber structure is the key of this manuscript. In this manuscript, by constructing asymmetric structure, SPR effect and MZ interference coexist in one sensing structure, the details are following: the sensing diagram is presented in Fig. 1 , which comprises a halogen light source with a wavelength changing from 200 nm to 1100 nm, proposed sensing structure, the optical spectrum analyzer (Ocean optics, USB4000), and computer. The computer is adopted for collecting and processing the spectra data from optical spectrum analyzer.

The sensing part composes of two sections of MMF and PCF with a length of 1.5 cm in the middle, the end face of PCF is shown in Fig. 1 (e), the outer diameter and air hole diameter of PCF are 125 μm and 4.8 μm respectively, and 7.7 μm is the distance between two adjacent air holes, as shown in Fig. 1 (e). The core and cladding diameters of MMF are 62.5 μm and 125 μm respectively.

When the light from light source transmits to the sensing structure, at the first point of MMF and PCF, part of light will enter the cladding of PCF for transmission, and then the evanescent wave will be formed at the interface between the gold film and optical fiber cladding, and the evanescent wave can be expressed as:
(1)
K x
=
ω c
ε 0
sin θ
c
is the speed of light in vacuum,
ω
is the angular frequency of light wave,
θ
is the angle of incidence,
ε 0
is the dielectric constant of fiber core.

The energy of evanescent wave will be transmitted in gold film and attenuated exponentially. This energy will stimulate the movement of free electrons in gold film and produce surface plasmon wave, which can be expressed as:
(2)
K
sp
=
ω c
ε 1
ε 2
ε 1
+
ε 2
ε 1
is the dielectric constant of the metal film,
ε 2
is the environment dielectric constant.

When the evanescent wave matches with the SPW and resonates that is
(3)
ω c
ε 0
sin
θ
spr
=
ω c
ε 1
ε 2
ε 1
+
ε 2

The metal will absorb part of the energy of the incident light, reducing the energy of the incident light, resulting in the SPR resonance absorption peak during monitoring in the spectrometer. SPR spectrum can be expressed as:
(4)
λ
sp
=
λ 0
ε 2
1 +
ε 2
ε 1 ′
=
λ 0
1
ε 2
+
1
ε 1 ′
Where
λ 0
is incident wavelength, When the ambient temperature or RH changes, the dielectric constant of the environment changes, which will eventually lead to the shift of SPR resonance peak, and the measurand can be characterized by observing the position of the formant.

In the side of PCF without Au coating, the light transmitted in the hollow hole of PCF is not affected by the change of external environment. The effective refractive index of cladding between PCF core holes is affected by the external environment. Therefore, the light transmitted out of PCF hollow hole is easily affected by the external environment. When the two beams arrive at the second fusion point of MMF and PCF, the two beams converge and interfere, and the interference light can be expressed as:
(5)
I = I 1 + I 2 + 2
I 1 I 2
cos
( Δ φ )
I 1
and
I 2
are the intensity of two transmitted beams,
Δ φ
is phase difference of two beams of light. When the external environment changes, the phase difference can be expressed as:
(6)
Δ φ =
2 π
Δ n e f f
L
λ
Δ
n
eff
is the difference of effective refractive index between fiber core (
n
eff
core
) and fiber cladding (
n
eff
clad
), and
λ
presents input light wavelength, L presents length of interference. When the phase difference between core mode and cladding mode is an odd multiple of π, that is
Δ φ = ( 2 m + 1 ) π
, and the m-order interference can be expressed as:
(7)
λ m =
2 Δ n e f f
L
2 m + 1

When relative humidity increases, the refractive index of sensitive materials will decrease, or the refractive index of sensitive materials will increase with temperature increasing, which will affect the refractive index of PCF cladding, and then the effective index difference will change, finally result to the spectrum shift. According to formula (4) and (7), the shift of the spectrum can be used to characterize the measured change.

In this manuscript, Au film is adopted to stimulate SPR effect, according to the principle of SPR, the evanescent wave has a certain penetration length, and SPR can be excited when the gold film thickness is 30 ∼ 50 nm. In this manuscript, the vacuum ion beam sputtering instrument (JS1600) is adopted for gold coating, and the thickness of gold film is controlled by coating time. The spectrum of proposed sensor with different coating time is presented in Fig. 2 . Fig. 2 reveals that the peak position of the spectrum changes little at different coating time, while the intensity changes obviously.

The spectrum of the sensor under the same salinity at different coating time is shown in Fig. 3 , which illustrates the spectrum has obvious differences, the spectral contrast with 2mian45s coating is the best.

As a mixture of GQDs (XF215, purchased from Nanjing/Jiangshu XFNANO materials Tech Co., Ltd, Jiangshu province, China) and PVA. Compared with other relative humidity sensitive materials, GQDs and PVA have good humidity response characteristics [23,24], in addition, GQDs-PVA has good humidity sensitivity and has been proved in our previous work [9,15,25]. The depositing process of GQDs-PVA are presented in Fig. 4 : Firstly, prepare a clean beaker and add PVA and GQDs in turn, the ratio of PVA and GQDs is 0.06. Coating GQDs-PVA composite on the surface of PCF coated with Au film by adjusting the speed of drawing machine. After coating process, the prepared sensor should be stored at 30 °C for 4 h. After the above steps, GQDs-PVA is uniformly deposited on proposed sensing structure. The proposed sensor with 2min45s Au coating time and 3.72 μm GQDs-PVA film coated is adopted for relative humidity and temperature experiments.

The proposed sensor with 2min45s Au coating time and GQDs-PVA film coated is adopted for relative humidity and temperature experiments. The sensing system is shown in Fig. 5 , which includes halogen lamp as light source, liquid tank (containing different saturated salt solutions) for relative humidity environment, ocean spectrometer and computer for spectrum collection and demodulation, and hygrometer for real-time monitoring of relative humidity value in liquid tank. In this experiment, the thickness of GQDs-PVA is 3.72 μm, and the relative humidity range is 22.97 %RH-85.64 %RH.

During RH experiment, keep temperature constant at 22℃. Under different relative humidity values, the spectrum corresponding to the sensing structure is shown in Fig. 6 (a), which reveals the valley value near 590 nm shifts toward red wavelength with the increase of relative humidity value, while the valley value near 690 nm shifts to blue wavelength direction with the increase of relative humidity value. The specific relationship between relative humidity value and valley shift is presented in Fig. 6 (b), the valley values around 590 nm and 690 nm are SPR and MZ interference spectrum. With the increase of relative humidity, the refractive index of GQDs-PVA decreases, according to formula (4) and (7), the spectrum of SPR moves in the direction of red light, the spectrum of MZ interference moves in the direction of blue light. The relative humidity experiment results in Fig. 6 reveals that the valley near 590 nm is 0.007 nm/%RH (linearity is 0.975), and the valley sensitivity to relative humidity near 690 nm is −0.023 nm%RH (linearity is 0.991).

About relative humidity stability, the relative humidity stability is mainly related to relative humidity sensitive materials, and our previous work [23] has verified that GQDs-PVA has good stability. In order to verify the stability of proposed sensor in this manuscript, the long-term stability experiment is performed, and the results is presented in Fig. 7 .

When the relative humidity value is 22.97 %RH, the maximum shift of the spectrum is equivalent to the change of relative humidity value by 2.6 %RH. When the relative humidity value is 55.92 %RH, the maximum shift of the spectrum is equivalent to the change of relative humidity value by 2.6 %RH, which is mainly due to temperature changes or system errors.

Temperature is a factor that must be considered in the process of accurate relative humidity detection. Therefore, the sensing structure is placed in the incubator for temperature experiment. During temperature experiment, keep RH constant at 25 %RH. Under different temperature values, the spectrum corresponding to the sensing structure is shown in Fig. 8 (a), which reveals that valley value near 590 nm moves to the short wavelength direction with temperature increasing, while the valley value near 690 nm moves to blue wavelength direction with temperature increasing. The specific relationship between temperature and valley value movement is presented in Fig. 8 (b), which illustrates that the sensitivity of valley value near 590 nm to temperature is −0.102 nm/℃ (linearity is 0.994), and the sensitivity of valley value near 690 nm to temperature is 0.286 nm/℃ (linearity is 0.973).

The experimental results of relative humidity and temperature show that the sensor has certain response characteristics to both relative humidity and temperature. Therefore, we can demodulate both relative humidity and temperature by establishing a sensitivity matrix. The sensitivity matrix is as follows:
(8)
Δ
λ 1
Δ
λ 2
=
S
1 - RH
S
1 - T
S
2 - RH
S
2 - T
Δ R H
Δ T
=
0.007
-
0.102
-
0.023
0.286
Δ R H
Δ T

Δ
λ 1
,
Δ
λ 2
are the moving amount of wave trough near 590 nm and 690 nm under temperature and relative humidity, respectively:
(9)
Δ R H
Δ T
= -
1
0.000344
0.286
-
0.023
-
0.102
0.007
Δ
λ 1
Δ
λ 2
=
- 831.395
66.860
296.512
-
20.231
Δ
λ 1
Δ
λ 2

When the temperature and relative humidity change at the same time, we will bring the corresponding wavelength change into the above formula, so as to realize the detection of relative humidity without temperature crosstalk.

In addition, the selectivity and response time are key elements that should be considered in application. Z.S. Hosseini and his team [24] has proved that GQDs is no obvious to response to CO, H2, CH4, and CO2. In terms of response time, the RH environment in this manuscript is offered by different saturated salt solutions which takes about 20 min achieving stable RH value, which makes it difficult to record the response time to RH. While the response time of GQDs and PVA to RH are 12 s and 600 ms respectively [27–28], therefore, GQDs-PVA as relative humidity sensitive mixture, the response time to relative humidity is fast, and accurate calculation of its response time to relative humidity will be the focus of our work in the future.

In order to demonstrate the sensing performance of the proposed RH sensor, the detailed parameter indexes of different recently reported optical fiber humidity sensors based on FBG, Sagnac interferometer, MZ interferometer [31] and Fabry Perot (FP) interferometer, the sensing character, such as the sensitivity, measurement range, humidity-sensitive materials and temperature crosstalk, are listed in Table 1 . Sagnac interferometer has the best sensitivity, while the volume of the Sagnac interferometer is relatively large, at least a few centimeters in diameter, which limits its application. Although the proposed sensor in this manuscript is not the most sensitive, it can overcome the influence of temperature which is not the performance of other most relative humidity sensors. In addition, the proposed sensor in this manuscript has wide measurement range.

A relative humidity sensor based on SPR formed in MZ interference structure is proposed in this manuscript, which overcomes the problem of temperature crosstalk effectively. The sensing structure is made by PCF and MMF to formed MZ interference, and the half of PCF coated with Au film forms SPR. GQDs-PVA acts RH sensing material coated on the surface of Au film. The sensitivity of proposed sensor to relative humidity is 23 pm/%RH. This manuscript presents a novel method to overcome the problem of temperature crosstalk in the process of relative humidity measurement, which can effectively avoid the complexity of the later data demodulation, the sensing structure is compact size and low cost. Additional instructions, the sensing method can be replicated to other sensors without crosstalk.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work is supported in part by the Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities (N2123014), in part by Natural Science Foundation of Hebei Province (F2020501040 and F2021501019) in China.
